behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is not just JSON.parse; it verifies parser output is accepted by downstream hook-input handling. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real process/OS I/O; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML step is present.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser + hook input handler in-process, feed valid JSON fixtures with code fences/backticks/$vars, assert parse succeeds and handler continues on normal path with no parse-failure trace.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at parser-to-trace boundary (error details must propagate into trace context). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network requirement in behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior does not require real stdin/process lifecycle; it only requires malformed input leading to trace content.
behaviors.test_description: In-process test over parser + trace-emitter modules: send malformed JSON cases, assert `json_parse_failed` is emitted and includes diagnostic context (e.g., offset/snippet/path) sufficient to locate malformed section.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is a local mapping rule (parse error -> handled result + telemetry intent), which can fail within one function/object boundary. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text 'local error-policy path' and explicit no-throw mapping semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Call the error-policy function/object directly with a representative parse-failure error; assert returned handled status, no exception thrown, and telemetry-intent flag is set.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit real process execution, stdin behavior, crash/exit semantics, and emission before exit require subprocess + OS pipe/exit handling. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can miss pipe buffering/order/termination issues. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified by behavior text 'In real process execution' and 'before exit'.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the real hook executable as a subprocess, write malformed stdin, capture exit code and trace output/artifact, assert no crash and expected trace signal is emitted prior to process termination.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires real invocation path from stdin through parser to sink artifact, which is an end-to-end process/I-O boundary. MOCK-HIDE: mocked stdin/sink can hide serialization/flush/path issues. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified by explicit 'real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute hook through its real entrypoint with malformed stdin and configured trace sink, then assert produced artifact contains expected parse-failure trace event and metadata.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a cross-path classification regression (valid markdown JSON vs malformed JSON) across parser/error-classification/trace handling modules. REAL-INFRA: not required by text; can be proven in-process. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior does not require real subprocess/OS lifecycle, only correct distinction between two input classes.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterized in-process regression suite over normal parse path and parse-failure path: run paired fixtures (valid markdown-heavy JSON, malformed JSON) and assert distinct outcomes/signals so markdown content is never labeled as parse failure.
